dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, my loved one left weeks ago and never arrived at their destination. Nobody's seen them since.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The last anyone heard, they were camped {location_hint} when trouble hit. I found the campsite a week later: torn apart, supplies scattered, but no body. {target_npc} thinks the {enemy_type} might have taken them. I need someone who can follow {quest_item} they left along their route: markers they always leave when traveling.
dialogueChunks.outro: They're out there somewhere. I can feel it. I just need someone brave enough to {quest_action}. {quest_origin} started all of this. {quest_reward_hint} would be my undying gratitude.

playerResponses.text: Weeks is a long time, but hope isn't lost. I'll follow the trail.
Tracking someone through hostile territory is dangerous work. What can you offer?
After this long, the odds of finding them alive are slim. I'm sorry.

dialogueChunks.intro: Please, {player_name}, you have to help me. My child wandered off during the {enemy_type} attack and nobody's seen them since.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When the {enemy_type} hit, everyone scattered. In the chaos, my child was separated from the group. {target_npc} saw them running toward {location_hint}, but nobody could follow because the {enemy_type} were between us. I need you to find {quest_item} they were carrying: anything that shows which direction they went.
dialogueChunks.outro: They're young and alone in the wild. Every hour counts. {quest_stakes} is all I can think about. {quest_time_pressure}.

playerResponses.text: A child alone out there? I'm already moving. Which direction?
I'll find them. Try to stay calm until I get back.
The wilderness is crawling with threats after that attack. I can't guarantee anything.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I've been looking for someone for a very long time. And I think I've finally found a lead.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} and I built this place together. Years ago, they left to establish a route through {location_hint} and promised to return. That promise turned into years of silence. Last week, a trader recognized {quest_item} I described: said they saw it in a camp near the edge of {enemy_type} territory.
dialogueChunks.outro: {target_npc} might be alive, might be captive, might be something I haven't considered. Either way, I owe it to them to {quest_action}. {quest_stakes} demands I try. {quest_origin} bound us together.

playerResponses.text: Years of searching deserves a real answer. I'll find it.
Enemy territory with slim leads. Risky, but I understand the need. What's the pay?
After so long, you should prepare for the worst.
